channel.title: Comments for page "Making a Scene"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Making a Scene"

item.title: Re: Continuation
item.content:encoded:
You miiight be thinking Dr Gears?

I know Gears has been dealing with stuff too, but the retirement from the site is what I was referring to.

item.title: Re: Continuation
item.content:encoded:
TwistedGears has been dealing with life stuff from what I understand

You miiight be thinking Dr Gears?

Anyway, I totally forgot to edit this back when I changed the LtA hub saying it was discontinued. There isn't ever going to be any follow up, I'm not writing for the wiki anymore.

So, uh. Thanks for reminding me to edit this, I guess. Bye.

item.title: Re: Continuation
item.content:encoded:
When/if it gets finished, presumably.
TwistedGears has been dealing with life stuff from what I understand, so it's not likely to be anytime soon.

item.title: Continuation
item.content:encoded:
in the end there is a promise of a continuation.

is there any estimate when/if we will get to see it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a good read, but part of me thinks you confused the Yakuza (Japanese criminals) with the triad (Chinese criminals). I also fixed a couple of grammar mistakes for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cool having him shortcut to the destination!

And his smoking serves a purpose! Also cool!

Typo:

remained taught > remained taut

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great job TwistedGears, this gave me the Chiba City blues (in a good way).

Loved Navarro and the humor, and your writing really stood out, with bits such as "Legs melting, he pressed onward" contributing to the overall neo-noir/cyberpunk feel of the tale.

+1

Also, found this typo:

Despite the fact he hated himself for it this less than thirty minutes ago, Navarro had to smile at the last option.

One of those two words should be removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The first half of the writing was like night and day with the previous story. Which was day. It was flat and fast-paced, and I found myself dreading reading a long tale that wasn't holding up to your previous one.

Thankfully, the writing seemed to click once they started shooting the shit. Which of course makes me wonder if the first half was necessary. To be sure, I was kind of expecting part 2 to do more in-depth introducing of who's on Sigma-3 and what their mission is. Still, I like where this went, it's good to see Navarro in action, and I like the tie-in with Learning the Alphabet. That'll be fun. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was just great. I'm not really sure what more I can say about this. I love this kind of urban-fantasy-snark-noir.

Noting though that this team's work, like String Quartet's, also weaves this new MTF into existing narratives.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I mostly enjoyed this, although the impact was reduced by the slightly sprawling nature of the story. I think I'll postpone my upvote until I've read the rest of the team's entries.

A few typo-type comments:

Despite the fact he hated himself for it this less than thirty minutes ago

Should one of those two words not be there?

Navarro peaked his head back into the room

peeked, and I'd suggest "peeked back into the room" (the head is implicit)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this tale for the same reason I love Learning the Alphabet installments. You pack so much sarcasm and whimsy into the narration I often feel like drawing comparisons to Douglas Adams.

+1. Can't wait to see Navarro make another appearance in LTA.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels warm and fuzzy at home with Learning the Alphabet. I love Navarro.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wanted to upvote for the first half of this tale, because I thought it was an interesting and descriptive look into the anomalous world you're exploring. The second half, however, was confusing and unsatisfying, and just seems like a very strange choice for the story at large. Tentative downvote.
